metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: [Healthcare](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/index.html)

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: [Nehreen Al-Mahmoedi](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/nehreen-al-mahmoedi/index.html)

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: [David Bolscher](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/david-bolscher/index.html)

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: The well-being of healthcare staff has decreased slightly in the past year after years of progress, according to ABN AMRO's Sectoral Well-being Monitor. The mental health of healthcare staff in particular has deteriorated. To retain the scarce healthcare staff, healthcare providers must support healthcare providers with their mental burden by creating an open and safe working culture.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: The high workload, increasing (sexual) intimidation and heavy physical work are starting to take their toll on healthcare providers, according to ABN AMRO's Sectoral Wellbeing Monitor. The emotional burden is highest in the healthcare sector and also leaves its mark outside the working walls. The percentage of staff suffering from burnout is above average in the healthcare sector at 4.4 percent. A growing number of caregivers also experience the feeling of complete exhaustion from work.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: ## Well-being is essential for sustainable healthcare provision

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: The tightness on the labor market remains acute. There are almost 60,000 vacancies in healthcare. The sector will also be severely affected by the aging population. In addition to an increase in the number of elderly people requiring care, a large number of healthcare workers are about to retire within ten years. On the one hand, this leads to a higher workload, but on the other hand, it strengthens the position of healthcare providers. Due to the shortage of healthcare personnel, employees can easily switch to another employer. More than four in five healthcare employees think they can easily change employers, according to the Sectoral Welfare Monitor.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: To fill vacancies and retain scarce healthcare personnel, healthcare providers must invest in the well-being of their employees. Satisfied employees are less likely to leave an organization and good employers are more likely to attract new staff. In addition, higher well-being contributes to higher productivity because satisfied employees get more done and are sick less often.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: ## Progress on work-life balance and personal development

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: The Sectoral Wellbeing Monitor shows that the healthcare sector has managed to improve the work-life balance of its staff for the fourth year in a row, despite the inflexible nature of healthcare. For example, the percentage of employees who regularly work overtime will decrease slightly: from 25.2 percent in 2023 to 23.9 percent in 2024. Within the healthcare workforce, doctors and specialized nurses work the most overtime on average, with 36.3 percent regularly working overtime. In addition, there is more freedom to take leave and determine your own working hours. This is especially true for psychologists and physiotherapists. Caregivers, assistants and nurses, on the other hand, have relatively the least freedom in this regard.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: Furthermore, in the past year there has been more room for the personal development of healthcare staff. For example, almost 68 percent of employees followed a course or training related to their work. In addition, managers in the healthcare sector encouraged the skills of their staff more than in other sectors. This is in line with the expectation that healthcare staff must complete specific training courses, for example to maintain their BIG registration.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: ## Mental and physical health care providers are a concern

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: The figures from the Sectoral Wellbeing Monitor show that keeping healthcare staff healthy once again remains a challenge. The healthcare sector takes the crown with the highest absenteeism rate compared to other sectors. According to employees, the absenteeism rate increased from 6.3 percent in 2023 to 6.7 percent in 2024. Caregivers (9.1 percent), social workers (8.3 percent) and psychologists (7.4 percent) in particular had high absenteeism due to illness.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: In addition, healthcare staff experience their work as both physically demanding and emotionally demanding. The number of caregivers experiencing emotional exhaustion has increased by almost 10 percent since 2015. More than a quarter of healthcare staff often or always experience their work as emotionally demanding. This is especially true for psychologists; almost two in three psychologists regularly find their work emotionally demanding. Doctors and social workers follow with about 40 percent. This mental burden also leads to high absenteeism due to illness.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: The mental health of healthcare workers is also deteriorating in other areas. For example, the number of employees who often experience a feeling of complete exhaustion at work is increasing, interactions are experienced as demanding by a larger part of the workforce, and the number of employees with a feeling of emptiness after a working day is increasing. The percentage of staff suffering from burnout is above average in the healthcare sector at 4.4 percent. To prevent dropout, absenteeism and burnout, an approach is needed to support healthcare providers with their heavy mental burden.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: ## Healthcare staff are still too often in unsafe situations

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: The safety of healthcare staff is the worst compared to all other sectors. Unfortunately, little progress has been made in the field of safety. The findings of our analysis last year therefore remain relevant. Nearly one in five healthcare workers has been intimidated or threatened by clients or patients in the past year. One in ten healthcare workers has experienced physical violence in the past year alone. Social workers in particular are often victims of physical violence, with one in five having experienced physical violence.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: Unwanted sexual attention is also remarkably high in healthcare. Nearly eight percent of healthcare personnel have had to deal with unwanted sexual attention from clients, patients or their loved ones, while the average percentage in other sectors is three percent. This percentage was the highest for nurses and doctors, at approximately fourteen percent. This is followed by nurses and physiotherapists, where one in ten is confronted with unwanted sexual attention.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: [Read also: Healthcare personnel also need care themselves](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/zorgpersoneel-heeft-zelf-ook-zorg-Need.html)

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: ## Open and safe working culture as a basis for healthy healthcare providers

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: Providing care can be difficult in many areas. The working culture in a hospital, nursing home or rehabilitation center where healthcare providers work often determines how much they want to work, stay or leave there. [research](https://www.trimbos.nl/aanbod/webwinkel/af1880-voorkomen-van-mentale- complaints-bij-zorgwerkers/) by the Trimbos Institute shows that the mental health of healthcare providers mainly benefits from an open and safe working culture. According to 77 percent of them, if caregivers can speak freely about workload, mentally demanding tasks and bullying behavior, this can help prevent tension and stress complaints. Discussing topics such as intimidation and inappropriate behavior can be taboo: it is still too often seen as a self-evident part of the job.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: Nearly 60 percent of healthcare providers also indicate that the ability to hire sufficient staff helps prevent mental complaints. Logically, sufficient staff leads to a lower workload, which can reduce the mental burden. In addition, it is important to provide space for healthcare workers to support each other. For example, a debriefing with colleagues after resuscitation can somewhat relieve the tension and stress of the intense moment.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: In addition, signing the [care manifesto](https://www.rcgog.nl/het-zorgmanifest) against sexual misconduct can be a step in the right direction for healthcare providers to support their employees. The joint approach to this undesirable behavior offers healthcare providers tools to initiate a cultural change. Awareness is essential, followed by drawing up codes of conduct, protocols and process improvements at various layers of an organization. Signing the manifesto is a clear signal to healthcare staff that transgressive behavior has no place in healthcare.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: Finally, a safe working atmosphere requires an occupational health and safety policy that not only focuses on the process after incidents and intimidation, but also includes proactive measures to prevent these. Important elements here are offering resilience training against aggression, setting up a reporting point for unsafe situations and facilitating measures against intimidating and violent behavior.

metadata.source_title: Caregivers are more often mentally exhausted

text: In the coming years, the sector will change to provide more care with fewer resources. The demand for care continues to increase due to an aging population, while it is hardly possible to attract more healthcare personnel. Innovation and collaboration make work easier and more fun, ensuring that healthcare provision remains sustainable and of high quality.
